models: **Kimi K3 resets the open-weight frontier.** Moonshot reports a 2.8T sparse MoE, native vision, 1M context, and 99% of Fable 5 across 14 overlapping launch-suite evaluations; weights are promised by July 27.
**GPT-5.6 now spans Sol, Terra and Luna**, giving leaders a deliberate capability, balanced, and high-throughput ladder under one family.
**GPT-5.5 remains active in Codex and the API.** It is retained as a compatibility and portfolio option rather than being hidden by the newer 5.6 family.
**Claude Haiku 4.5 remains Anthropic’s latest verified Haiku.** No official Haiku 5 listing was found in Anthropic’s current model catalog.
harnesses: **Separate model capability from harness capability.** Tool execution, context management, isolation, and observability can dominate real workflow outcomes.
**Subscription access is not a production routing contract.** Validate API, credit-pool, and third-party harness policies before standardizing an operating model.
**Maintain at least one portable fallback path** across providers for high-value workflows and operational incidents.
self_hosting: **Open weights are now a strategic option, not only a cost play.** Kimi K3, DeepSeek, Qwen, Nemotron, Mistral, and Llama cover different sovereignty and specialization needs.
**Do not compare self-hosting at zero token cost.** Include accelerator rental or depreciation, power, utilization, serving staff, and measured throughput.
**Pilot against a defined workload and hardware envelope** before treating advertised context or parameter scale as deployable capacity.
strategy: **Run a portfolio, not a winner-takes-all model standard.** Reserve frontier reasoning for high-value decisions and route routine work to measured fast or efficient tiers.
**Instrument quality, latency, retries, and total workflow cost together.** Token price alone is not an operating metric.
**Review the portfolio quarterly and after major releases**, with explicit retirement, security, and fallback criteria.

changelog.text: Refreshed five open agent harnesses from their canonical GitHub releases: Codex CLI 0.144.6, Gemini CLI 0.51.0, OpenCode 1.18.4, Cline 4.0.10, and Goose 1.43.0; updated repository star snapshots and notable release capabilities.
Added Moonshot's official Kimi K3 API pricing: $3/M uncached input, $0.30/M cached input, and $15/M output; the 30/70 workload blend is $11.40/M before reasoning-effort effects.
Added Kimi K3 from Moonshot primary sources: 2.8T sparse MoE, 1M context, native vision, max-only thinking at launch, API availability, and a vendor-suite quality comparison against Fable 5.

Restored GPT-5.5 family visibility, verified Haiku 4.5 as the latest public Haiku, replaced quality compound display with quality vs selected reference, and removed non-actionable headline cost/policy counters.

Added source-grounded GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra and Luna pricing, 1.05M context, benchmark registers and selectable reference configurations. Added documented quality, speed, cost and capability composites in data/report-metrics.json.
Updated the action queue and recommended routing: Fable for hardest retained-data workloads, Terra for default engineering, Luna for high-volume subagents, with explicit escalation rules.

Restored GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark (Feb 12, 2026 release; ChatGPT Pro research preview, 128K context, 1000+ tok/s on Cerebras) and Hermes Agent v0.16.0 (Nous Research, MIT, self-hosted multi-platform agent) — both were incorrectly removed in v1.5.0 sweep.

Dashboard market sweep v1.5.0: real-world re-grounding. Replaced fictional Mythos/GPT-5.5-Cyber rows with verified models; added Nvidia Nemotron coalition, Kimi K2.6, GLM-5, Cohere Command A+, SubQ 1M-Preview.
Nvidia releases Nemotron 3 Ultra (550B/55B MoE, hybrid Mamba-Transformer, 1M context, NVIDIA Open Model License) at Computex — first frontier-scale open model from Nvidia.
Nvidia Nemotron Coalition formed: Black Forest Labs, Cursor, LangChain, Mistral, Perplexity, Reflection AI, Sarvam, Thinking Machines Lab as inaugural members.
Nvidia Cosmos 3 launched — open physical-AI / robotics foundation model.
Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.8 — flagship. SWE-bench Verified 88.6%, SWE-Pro 69.2%, AAII 61.4. Same headline $5/$25 but Fast Mode drops 3× to $10/$50.
Gemini CLI v0.44.0 — last major release before transition to Antigravity CLI on June 18 (free tier).
Codex CLI v0.134.0 — conversation-history search, iOS notification handoff, /archive, Windows elevated sandbox.
DeepSeek V4-Pro hits permanent pricing $0.435/$0.87 per MTok — roughly Opus-class quality at ~1/10 the cost.
Cohere ships Command A+ — first open-weights Cohere release in >1 year (218B Sparse-MoE multimodal).
Cursor 3.5 — Cloud Agents (isolated VMs, multi-repo), Composer 2.5, Agents Window.
Google I/O — Gemini 3.5 Flash ships GA at $1.50/$9 per MTok; claims frontier-grade quality + 4× faster than peers.
Anthropic announces billing restructure (effective June 15, 2026): Pro/Max split into Chat pool + separate Agent SDK credit pool ($20/$100/$200).
xAI releases Grok 4.3 at $1.25/$2.50 per MTok with 1M context, hybrid reasoning, multimodal — aggressive pricing for frontier tier.

Capability radar v0.3.1: render fix (inline fill/stroke on every SVG primitive so the chart no longer collapses into one dark hexagon when CSS isn't loaded). Editorial-instrument redesign per frontend-design plugin: compass-rose graticules, composite score as centerpiece (large serif italic), per-axis sublabels showing current level name, focused-spoke red highlight, leaderboard styled as data-table. Removed superfluous task-fit-recommendations section on 04 Strategy (the radar superseded its purpose).

Capability radar v0.3.0: new 6-axis radar chart on 04 Strategy (Coding · Reasoning & Architecture · Knowledge & Research · Communication & Docs · Multimodal · Agentic), with weighted composite scoring + 7 focus presets (Balanced + one per axis at weight 2.0). Each model rated 1-5 per axis against a frozen rubric (see src/dashboard-context.md); seeded all 19 frontier+fast models. Agentic axis is model-side only (harness scoring stays on 02 Harnesses). Pure-SVG render, no chart-library dep.

Grounded GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark from OpenAI primary sources: 128K context, 1000+ tok/s, Codex app/CLI/VS Code research preview, separate preview quota; burn matrix now encodes zero standard-pool burn instead of blank cells pending final credit rates.

Dashboard v9: added cache hit/miss modeling. Per-model cache_discount field (Anthropic 10%, OpenAI 25%, DeepSeek 0.83-2%, MiniMax 52%, Mistral none). Four workload presets: Cold/Mixed/Warm/Hot. Burn matrix and Efficiency Leaders both honor the selected workload.

Dashboard v8: quality & burn matrix validation. Anthropic high/xhigh/max burns increased to match thinking-token budgets (DataCamp/ArtificialAnalysis); OpenAI xhigh raised to 3.5× medium (nxcode); quality factors refined per Hex/ampcode measurements. Added Mythos Preview row.
Claude for Legal launches (TR, LexisNexis, Harvey, Legora integrations)
Anthropic + SAP partnership: Claude embedded across SAP Business AI Platform
Dashboard market sweep v1.4.0: corrected Opus 4.7 pricing ($5/$25, not $15/$75), SWE-Pro 64.3%; refreshed open-weight roster
Claude Platform on AWS launched — Anthropic-managed infrastructure with AWS billing/IAM
Claude Code adds agent view and /goal command (multi-turn completion conditions)
Dashboard v1.3.0: Strategy tab adds Task-fit Recommendations — 10 task types × 4 providers, with runners-up surfaced on click
Dashboard v1.2.1: burn baseline now follows the reference dropdown — switching reference recomputes all burn × ratios against the chosen model
Dashboard v1.2.0: burn matrix cells now show burn + quality together (E2 encoding); efficiency leaders panel added
Corrected Anthropic burn matrix: Claude Code uses /effort low/medium/high/xhigh/max (adaptive thinking), not legacy budget_tokens
OpenAI releases GPT-5.5-Cyber to vetted security teams (parallel to Mythos)
Gemini 3.2 Flash leaked at $0.25/$2 per Mtok; expected May 19-20 at Google I/O
OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT default (replaces GPT-5.3 Instant)
Anthropic releases Opus 4.7 with extended 1M context and improved long-context retention

actions: P0 · Executive sponsor — define three transformation outcomes with measurable business and engineering baselines; avoid scaling pilots that have no accountable owner or adoption target.
P0 · Technology leadership — establish a model portfolio policy with capability, data-classification, regional, fallback, and retirement rules instead of standardizing on one provider.
P0 · Platform and finance — instrument end-to-end quality, latency, retries, human rework, and cost for representative workflows before negotiating capacity or subscriptions.
P1 · Security and legal — approve reusable controls for retention, training use, tool permissions, audit evidence, and human escalation by data class.
P1 · Engineering leadership — run a 30-task quarterly evaluation across one frontier, one balanced, one fast, and one open-weight route using identical harness conditions.
P2 · Infrastructure — select one sovereignty or resilience workload for an open-weight pilot and publish its full hardware, utilization, staffing, and throughput economics.

models.name: Claude Fable 5
models.subscription: Claude API and supported cloud platforms
models.notes: Default reference for v2. Anthropic describes Fable 5 as its most capable widely released model. Adaptive thinking is always on. Comparative latency is slower. Retention is 30 days and zero-data-retention is not available.

models.name: GPT-5.6 Sol
models.notes: Flagship GPT-5.6 tier. Official benchmark table and pricing; speed is stored as an end-to-end task index in report-metrics.json, not tok/s.

models.name: GPT-5.6 Terra
models.notes: Balanced GPT-5.6 tier and recommended default engineering route. Comparable task-speed evidence is indexed in report-metrics.json.

models.name: GPT-5.6 Luna
models.notes: Fastest, most affordable GPT-5.6 tier; recommended for high-volume subagents with verification and escalation.

models.name: Kimi K3
models.license: open weights announced; terms pending weight release
models.subscription: Kimi API, Kimi Code, Kimi Work
models.notes: 2.8T sparse MoE with 16/896 experts active, native vision and always-on thinking. API pricing is $3/M uncached input, $0.30/M cached input and $15/M output. Full weights promised by July 27, 2026.
models.quality_evidence: Geometric mean across 14 overlapping values in Moonshot’s launch comparison; vendor-reported, max/xhigh settings.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.8
models.notes: Current Anthropic flagship. SWE-V 88.6%, SWE-Pro 69.2%, AAII 61.4. Fast Mode reduced 3× to $10/$50 (was $30/$150 on 4.7). 1M context standard.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.7
models.notes: Now legacy as of Opus 4.8 release May 28. Pricing unchanged. SWE-Verified 87.6%, SWE-Pro 64.3%.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
models.notes: Best code style/intent understanding. With cache+batch: $0.30/$7.50 effective.

models.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
models.notes: 5× cheaper than Sonnet. Triage/classification champion.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.notes: OpenAI flagship; default in ChatGPT (Instant variant since May 5, 2026). 1M context with 2× input/1.5× output surcharge above 272K. Reasoning tokens billed as output.

models.name: GPT-5.5 Pro
models.notes: Highest-stakes reasoning tier. $30/$180. Available in ChatGPT Pro $200 and as API. 6× cost of base 5.5.

models.name: GPT-5.4
models.notes: Best quality-per-credit. Held SWE-Pro lead Feb-April. Default for everyday coding.

models.name: GPT-5.4 Mini
models.notes: 94% of GPT-5.4 coding at 6× less. Best subagent. ~1/20 burn vs GPT-5.5. Collapses at 64K+ context.

models.name: GPT-5.4 Nano
models.notes: Smallest reasoning model. API-only. For embeddable/edge inference at near-zero cost.

models.name: GPT-5.3-Codex
models.subscription: All paid tiers · Code Review uses this
models.notes: Coding-specialised. ~⅓ burn vs GPT-5.5 for ~2pts less SWE-Pro. Often best $/quality for execution turns.

models.name: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark
models.notes: Smaller, latency-first sibling of GPT-5.3-Codex. Released Feb 12, 2026. 1000+ tok/s on Cerebras hardware. ChatGPT Pro research preview only — not in API at launch; separate preview rate-limit pool (no standard credit burn). Text-only. Target use: real-time micro-edits, live pair-programming in Codex app/CLI/VS Code.

models.name: GPT-5.2
models.notes: Codex picker keeps it for 'long-running agents' (specifically tuned for autonomy). Otherwise eclipsed by 5.3-Codex.

models.name: GPT-5.2-Codex
models.notes: Predecessor to 5.3-Codex. Legacy.

models.name: GPT-5.5 Instant
models.notes: ChatGPT default since May 5, 2026. 52.5% fewer hallucinations vs 5.3, 30% shorter responses, first Instant-class High-capability rating on cybersec/bio-chem.

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.notes: Released Feb 19, 2026 (preview). Pricing doubles above 200K input tokens. 50% batch discount.

models.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
models.notes: Shipped GA at Google I/O May 19, 2026. Frontier-grade quality, 4× faster than peers per Google.

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite
models.notes: Lite tier; 1M context retained.

models.name: DeepSeek V4-Pro
models.notes: MIT-licensed. Permanent pricing May 22, 2026 — Opus-class quality at ~1/10 cost. 1.6T/49B MoE, 1M context.

models.name: DeepSeek V4-Flash
models.notes: 284B/13B active MoE. Cheap large-context option. MIT license.

models.name: MiniMax M2.7
models.notes: Current flagship reasoner; MoE 230B/10B active.

models.name: Grok 4.3
models.notes: Current xAI flagship — aggressive pricing for frontier tier. Hybrid reasoning. Multimodal text+image.

models.name: Grok 4.1 Fast
models.notes: Cheapest large-context model on market. 2M context, $0.05/Mtok cached input.

models.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
models.notes: EU jurisdiction. 128B dense, Apache 2.0. Strongest non-Chinese open-weight coding agent. Vibe agents (GitHub/Linear/Jira/Sentry integrations). Pricing not verified; Medium 3 was $0.40/$2.00.

models.name: Mistral Large 3
models.notes: Cheapest premium output price in market among Western frontier. 675B/41B MoE, Apache 2.0.

models.name: SubQ 1M-Preview
models.notes: First commercial subquadratic (non-transformer) LLM. ~1/5 frontier cost on long-context tasks. Capability rating estimated — public benchmarks pending.

models.name: Nvidia Nemotron 3 Ultra
models.notes: 550B/55B MoE, hybrid Mamba-Transformer. ~300 tok/s. Open weights also available — see self_hosting. Capability levels are best estimates pending independent benchmarks.

subscriptions.features: Chat only (Claude Code removed April 2026). From June 15, 2026 split into Chat pool + Agent SDK credit pool.

subscriptions.limits: 5× Pro quotas · ~225 msg/5h Sonnet · expanded Opus
subscriptions.features: Cache reads included flat-rate. From June 15, 2026: Chat pool + separate Agent SDK credit pool.
For heavy Opus users. From June 15, 2026: Chat + Agent SDK credit pools.
Cheapest frontier API ($0.435/$0.87 permanent since May 22, 2026)

agent_policies.first_party_exception: `claude -p` pipe mode and Claude Code itself

harnesses.name: Claude Code
harnesses.sweet_spot: SubagentStop hooks, /plugin list, requiredMinimumVersion managed setting, MCP fixes, Agent Teams.
harnesses.stumbles: Anthropic-only. Removed from standard Pro tier April 2026 — push to Max.

harnesses.name: OpenCode
providers: 75+: Anthropic*, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, Kimi, GLM, Ollama, LM Studio
harnesses.sweet_spot: v1.18.4 (Jul 20, 2026): adaptive Kimi thinking controls, provider-defined reasoning options, restored Azure endpoints, and a rewritten desktop prompt input.
harnesses.stumbles: Anthropic OAuth blocked April 4 — must use API key.

harnesses.name: Codex CLI
harnesses.sweet_spot: v0.144.6 (Jul 18, 2026): refreshed GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna bundled instructions and corrected Codex context-window metadata to 272K.
harnesses.stumbles: OpenAI-only. No MCP, no hooks. Tightly coupled to apply_patch tool.

harnesses.name: Gemini CLI
harnesses.sweet_spot: v0.51.0 (Jul 16, 2026): hardened sensitive-path and symlink handling, read-only macOS sandbox git config, and modern-model escape-sequence fixes.
harnesses.stumbles: Sunsetting to Antigravity CLI for free tier on June 18, 2026; paid Gemini/Enterprise keys retain access.

harnesses.name: Aider
harnesses.sweet_spot: Mature, lightweight, voice-input native. Pair-programmer mode. Active, last commit Mar 2026. 44k stars.
harnesses.stumbles: No MCP, no hooks. Less ambitious than newer harnesses.

harnesses.name: Cline
harnesses.sweet_spot: v4.0.10 (Jul 20, 2026): current release adds telemetry for consecutive-mistake-limit events; multi-editor and CLI surfaces remain available.
harnesses.stumbles: Anthropic OAuth blocked. Can be expensive on long sessions.

harnesses.name: Roo Code
harnesses.sweet_spot: v3.53.0 (Apr 23, 2026). Power-user Cline fork, model-agnostic, BYOK. Recent: GPT-5.5 via Codex, Opus 4.7 on Vertex, checkpoint nav.

harnesses.name: Cursor
harnesses.pricing: Hobby free · Pro $20 · Pro+ $60 · Ultra $200 · Teams $40/user
harnesses.sweet_spot: Cursor 3.5 (May 20, 2026): Cloud Agents (isolated VMs, multi-repo), Composer 2.5, Agents Window, parallel subagents.
harnesses.stumbles: Closed source. Lock-in. Subscription required for serious use.

harnesses.name: Windsurf / Devin Desktop
harnesses.sweet_spot: Renamed to Devin Desktop, Agent Command Center kanban, embedded Devin cloud agent, SWE-1.6 model, multi-agent + worktrees.

harnesses.name: Goose
harnesses.sweet_spot: v1.43.0 (Jul 14, 2026): per-message token/cost/TTFT/tok-s metrics, ACP reconnection, GPT-5.6 support, dynamic Ollama Cloud discovery, and expanded providers.
harnesses.stumbles: Anthropic OAuth blocked. Less mindshare than OpenCode.

harnesses.name: OMO (Multi-model orchestrator)
harnesses.sweet_spot: Rebrand from oh-my-opencode; multi-model orchestration. Wraps Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Kimi K2, DeepSeek V4, Gemini CLI.

harnesses.name: Hermes Agent
harnesses.sweet_spot: v0.16.0. Persistent memory + auto-generated skills — learns your projects. Bridges Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/Email/CLI. Natural-language cron for unattended runs. Parallel isolated subagents. Web search, browser automation, vision, image-gen, TTS.
harnesses.stumbles: Not coding-specialised — general autonomous agent. Setup requires self-host script. No first-party SWE benchmark.

harnesses.name: GitHub Copilot CLI
harnesses.sweet_spot: GA Feb 25, 2026. Specialized sub-agents (Explore, Task, Code Review, Plan), background delegation, autopilot.

harnesses.name: Amp CLI
harnesses.sweet_spot: Spun out as standalone company 2026. Runs as sidebar agent inside Zed via Terminal Threads.

harnesses.name: Zed
harnesses.sweet_spot: Rust-native editor with first-class agent panel + ACP host. Terminal Threads run Claude Code/Amp inline.
harnesses.stumbles: Editor first; agent layer still maturing.

harnesses.name: Continue
harnesses.sweet_spot: Agent mode plan+execute. Continuous AI, Mission Control, shared PR/ticket workflows.
harnesses.stumbles: Newer agent features still stabilizing across providers.

hardware_options.name: Vast.ai 2× A6000
hardware_options.notes: Reference cloud-GPU setup for this dashboard. No upfront capex. Docker templates, SSH/Cloudflare Zero Trust. Marketplace rates vary by host, region, reliability, storage, and rental type.

hardware_options.name: Vast.ai 3× A6000
hardware_options.notes: Headroom for Qwen 3 235B-A22B Q6_K. Marketplace rates vary by host, region, reliability, storage, and rental type.

hardware_options.name: Vast.ai 4× A6000
hardware_options.notes: Llama 3.1 405B Q4 territory. Marketplace rates vary by host, region, reliability, storage, and rental type.

hardware_options.name: MacBook Pro 14" M4 Pro 64GB
hardware_options.notes: MoE sweet spot. 273 GB/s memory bandwidth.

hardware_options.name: MacBook Air 15" 32GB
hardware_options.notes: Hard 32GB ceiling. Limited to ~14B dense or 26B MoE Q4.

hardware_options.name: Contabo 2 x L40S
hardware_options.notes: EU-oriented fixed monthly configuration; 64 vCPU, 213 GB RAM, 3.5 TB storage, and 15 TB bandwidth are listed for the 2-GPU tier.

hardware_options.name: Contabo 1 x H200 NVL
hardware_options.notes: Fixed monthly large-memory option. Confirm location and availability before treating it as a sovereignty or latency fit.

hardware_options.name: Infomaniak 1 x L40S
hardware_options.notes: Swiss OpenStack option with dedicated GPU access and usage billing. Public pages list L40S availability but do not expose a stable crawlable SKU price; validate availability for the selected region.

hardware_options.name: Hyperstack 1 x H200 SXM
hardware_options.notes: Minute-accurate on-demand billing; reservation pricing starts at $2.45/hr. Validate region, storage, and availability.

models.name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B MoE
models.notes: MoE — only 4B active per token. Faster than dense models 5× its size.

models.name: Gemma 4 31B Dense
models.notes: Highest quality open Gemma. Slower per-token (full 31B active).

models.name: Qwen 3.6 Plus
models.notes: 1M context. Top open agentic coder. April 11 release.

models.name: Llama 4 Maverick
models.notes: 400B / 17B active MoE. 1M context. Strong MMLU-Pro (80.5%) but coding behind Chinese labs.

models.name: MiniMax M2.5 (open weights)

models.name: DeepSeek V4-Pro (open weights)
models.notes: 1.6T total / 49B active MoE. MIT license. Strongest open coder. Requires very large multi-GPU deployment.

models.name: Llama 4 Scout
models.notes: 10M token context (longest in any open model). 109B / 17B active MoE.

models.name: Kimi K2.6
models.notes: Best open-weight for sub-agent fan-out. Built for harness-driven parallel pipelines. Chinese-trained.

models.name: GLM 5.1
models.notes: MIT license — rare among open frontier models besides DeepSeek. Strong for enterprise fine-tuning.

models.name: Mistral Small 4
models.notes: 6.5B effective parameters. EU jurisdiction. Best on-device option.

models.name: Nvidia Nemotron 3 Ultra
models.notes: Hybrid Mamba-Transformer. 1M context. NVIDIA Open Model License. Computex June 4 launch.

models.name: Nvidia Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3B
models.notes: Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Nano variant. Open weights.

models.name: Nvidia Nemotron Nano 9B v2
models.notes: Dense 9B. Strong instruction following at edge sizes.

models.name: Kimi K2.6 (1T MoE)
models.notes: Top open intelligence. 80.2% SWE-Verified, 58.6% SWE-Pro, AAII 54. 262K context. Modified MIT.

models.name: GLM 4.6
models.notes: MIT, 200K context. Z.ai release.

models.name: Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B
models.notes: Apache 2.0 MoE — strong $/quality for fast bulk inference.

models.name: Cohere Command A+
models.notes: First open-weights Cohere release in >1 year. Sparse-MoE multimodal.

models.name: Mistral Large 3 (open weights)
models.notes: 675B/41B active MoE. Apache 2.0. EU jurisdiction.

models.name: DeepSeek V4-Flash (open weights)
models.notes: MIT, 1M context, 384K max output. Cheap large-context open option.

frameworks.name: llama.cpp
frameworks.notes: Best Apple Silicon performance via Metal.

frameworks.name: Ollama
frameworks.notes: Wrapper around llama.cpp. One-line model pull.

current_recommendation.label: Risk-tiered GPT-5.6 + Fable stack
components: GPT-5.6 Luna low — high-volume subagents and routine transformations
GPT-5.6 Terra medium — default engineering, review and documentation
GPT-5.6 Sol high or Claude Fable 5 high — escalation for hard, long-horizon tasks
Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B or DeepSeek V4-Flash — local route for privacy-sensitive bulk work
current_recommendation.rationale: Route by task risk instead of one subscription. Terra and Luna now retain strong coding-agent quality at substantially lower list price; Fable remains the long-horizon option only where its 30-day retention requirement is acceptable. Monthly cost is workload-dependent and must be computed from measured token volume.

alternatives.label: Dual subscription (Claude Max + OpenAI Pro)
alternatives.rationale: Adds GPT-5.4 SWE-bench Pro lead and Codex CLI cloud sandbox. Worth $100/mo only if you frequently hit hard issues where Sonnet 4.6 plateaus.
alternatives.verdict: Defer until you have measured Sonnet plateau frequency for 30 days.

alternatives.label: API-only (no subscriptions)
alternatives.rationale: Pure pay-per-use. Maximum flexibility. Loses the subscription cache advantage — same workload costs 1.5–5× more for power users.
alternatives.verdict: Worse economics for your usage volume. Skip.

alternatives.label: Self-hosted maximalist
alternatives.rationale: Vast.ai 24/7 with Gemma 4 + Qwen 3 + occasional API top-up for frontier-only tasks.
alternatives.verdict: Cheapest if quality plateau at ~88% of Opus is acceptable. Operational overhead is real.

routing.use_for: Classification, simple edits, triage, log parsing
routing.preferred: GPT-5.6 Luna low · Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B when local
routing.cost_label: $1/$6 Mtok before cache, batch and reasoning tokens

routing.use_for: Multi-file edits, code review, refactors, docs
routing.cost_label: $2.50/$15 Mtok before cache, batch and reasoning tokens

routing.preferred: GPT-5.6 Sol high · Claude Fable 5 high for long-horizon autonomy
routing.cost_label: Sol $5/$30; Fable $10/$50 Mtok before reasoning tokens

open_questions: Will the Nvidia Nemotron Coalition (Mistral, Cursor, Black Forest Labs, Thinking Machines) actually deliver a frontier-open consortium, or fragment within a quarter?
How does the June 15 Anthropic billing restructure (Chat pool + Agent SDK credit pool) change Max economics for agentic workloads?
Does Gemini 3.5 Flash + free Antigravity CLI (post-June 18) reshape the free-tier hierarchy?
DeepSeek V4-Pro permanent pricing ($0.435/$0.87) — does it force closed providers (OpenAI, Anthropic) to cut headline prices?
SubQ 1M-Preview's subquadratic architecture — does the next year see other commercial non-transformer LLMs?

task_fit.description: For each task type, one recommended pick per provider drawn from the full roster. Click any cell to see runners-up that the daily briefing also considered. Burn × is derived from the matrix and recomputes when you change the reference dropdown.

rows.description: Single-file syntax fixes, regex replacements, formatting, docstrings, import sorting

google.rationale: Free tier covers 1000 req/day at 1M context

rows.description: Single-function implementation, straightforward bug fixes, simple feature additions

anthropic.rationale: Best $/quality on Anthropic side; cache reads included in Max 5×

openai.rationale: Coding-specialised, ~⅓ burn of GPT-5.5 for near-identical SWE-Pro

google.rationale: 1M context that works, generous free tier

self_hosted.rationale: Highest-quality open Gemma at full BF16 in 96GB

rows.description: Feature spanning 3-10 files, requires understanding cross-file context

anthropic.rationale: Best style/intent understanding for code spanning files

openai.rationale: Strong on planning; medium gives consistent multi-file edits

google.rationale: 1M context shines when many files are involved

self_hosted.rationale: Largest open MoE; strong reasoning across files

rows.description: Race conditions, performance bottlenecks, design decisions with long-term implications

anthropic.rationale: Default Claude Code effort for Opus; best long-context retention

openai.rationale: SWE-Pro leader; high effort balances depth and burn

google.rationale: Strongest Google option; 1M context for whole-repo analysis

self_hosted.rationale: Open-weight models trail frontier ~10-15pts here; not yet ready for hardest cases

rows.description: Overnight runs, agentic loops, tasks you don't supervise turn-by-turn

anthropic.rationale: Autonomy earns max effort's premium; no human in the loop to correct

openai.rationale: Highest available reasoning budget on OpenAI side

google.rationale: Gemini lacks max-equivalent for unsupervised work

self_hosted.rationale: Not recommended — frontier models earn their cost on the hardest tasks

anthropic: opus-4.7 @ xhigh — if max budget is too steep

rows.description: Reviewing diffs, suggesting improvements, finding issues without writing code yourself

anthropic.rationale: Critical thinking at moderate burn; doesn't need Opus depth

openai.rationale: Coding-tuned reviewer at ⅓ burn of GPT-5.5

google.rationale: Reviews benefit from long context across the PR

self_hosted.rationale: Critique tasks don't need frontier; quality plateau acceptable

rows.description: Cross-cutting changes across a large codebase, where retention is the bottleneck

anthropic.rationale: 1M context with retention that actually works; Opus's sweet spot

openai.rationale: 400K API context; degrades faster than Opus past 200K

self_hosted.rationale: Open models cap around 200K useful context; not yet competitive

openai: gpt-5.5 @ xhigh — if context fits in 256K

rows.description: Spawned by a main agent for focused work; quality threshold lower than user-facing

anthropic.rationale: Fast, cheap, no effort knob to manage

openai.rationale: Best subagent — 94% of GPT-5.4 coding at 6× less

google.rationale: Free tier perfect for fan-out subagents

self_hosted.rationale: Zero per-token cost for high-volume subagent loops

rows.description: READMEs, code comments, tutorials, explaining existing code in prose

anthropic.rationale: Best prose style on Anthropic side

openai.rationale: Reasoning helps less here; medium-effort GPT-5.4 wins on $/quality

google.rationale: Reasoning overkill; Gemini's prose competitive at low effort

self_hosted.rationale: Open models are competitive for prose tasks

rows.description: Boilerplate test files, fixture generation, mocking, parameterised test suites

anthropic.rationale: Pattern-following work; doesn't need reasoning depth

self_hosted.rationale: Bulk test scaffolding is exactly where self-hosted earns out

rows.description: Penetration testing, red-teaming, vulnerability discovery — tasks where cyber-specific safeguards apply

anthropic.rationale: Most capable Anthropic model; cyber tasks require Cyber Verification Program

openai.rationale: Flagship reasoning; vetted security access via OpenAI

self_hosted.rationale: Open-weight without safety filtering; deploy in isolated environment

quota_burn_matrix.baseline_label: selected reference model at medium effort = 1.00×
quota_burn_matrix.methodology: Burn ratios are displayed as multiples of the selected reference model at medium effort. Underlying values stored as absolute units anchored to gpt-5.5 medium = 1.00. Per-model ratios from API list pricing (firm, ±5%). Per-effort multipliers grounded in: Anthropic's published thinking-token budgets (low=skip, medium=~1k, high=5k, xhigh=10k, max=20k tokens); ArtificialAnalysis's measurement of Sonnet 4.6 max ≈ 3× Sonnet 4.5 on the Intelligence Index; nxcode.io / OpenAI guidance that xhigh ≈ 3-5× medium; ampcode's GPT-5.5 cost analysis. Per-effort multipliers are ±20%. NOTE: quality vs effort is not strictly monotonic per task — aggregate quality trends upward, but individual tasks can see high beat xhigh or medium beat high. OpenAI explicitly warns that 'high is not automatically better than medium'. Models that run in a separate preview quota bucket with no standard-pool burn are encoded as 0.00x and annotated until final token or credit rates are published.

openai_matrix.note: List-price blend equals the historical GPT-5.5 medium unit. Effort factors are planning estimates until measured token use is available.
Half Sol list price at every base token; effort factors remain estimates.
One-fifth Sol list price at every base token; effort factors remain estimates.

anthropic_matrix.note: Always-on adaptive thinking. Values start from the $10/$50 list-price blend; effort multipliers are planning estimates rather than published token budgets.
Current flagship. Same headline $5/$25. Fast Mode 3× cheaper ($10/$50) vs Opus 4.7.
Legacy as of May 28. Thinking tokens: low=skip, medium=~1k, high=5k, xhigh=10k, max=20k. New tokenizer +35% tokens vs 4.6.
API default high. No xhigh tier. AA measured max ≈ 3× Sonnet 4.5 cost.

quota_burn_matrix.non_reasoning_note: Models without reasoning controls: Haiku 4.5 (Anthropic matrix above as fixed rate), GPT-5.5 Instant (ChatGPT default since May 5), Mistral Medium 3.5, DeepSeek V4-Pro/Flash, MiniMax M2.7, Kimi K2.6, GLM 4.6, Qwen 3.6 variants, all Llama 4 variants, Grok 4.1 Fast, SubQ 1M-Preview — burn at fixed rate per model regardless of effort knob.
quota_burn_matrix.quality_methodology: Quality % = (model_swe_pro / reference_swe_pro × effort_quality_factor) × 100. Effort quality factors: minimal=0.65, low=0.85, medium=0.94, high=0.98, xhigh=1.00, max=1.02. Anchors: Anthropic's Hex measurement (low Opus 4.7 ≈ medium Opus 4.6 quality) anchors low ≈ 0.85; apiyi.com's report that max gains ~3pts over xhigh on hardest tasks anchors max=1.02; ampcode's GPT-5.5 analysis (medium captures 'most' of capability) anchors medium=0.94. IMPORTANT: these are AGGREGATE estimates. stet.sh found per-task reversals — high can beat xhigh on some tasks, medium can beat high. Treat as ±10% indicators, not precise measures.
quota_burn_matrix.unit_anchor_note: All burn values are stored as absolute units anchored to gpt-5.5 medium = 1.00. At render time, each cell is divided by the reference model's medium-effort cell (or its single datapoint for non-reasoning models) to produce the displayed ×-of-reference number. When the reference dropdown changes, all burn ratios recalculate against the new reference.

cold.label: Cold (0% cache)
cold.description: One-off prompts, no context reuse. Worst-case burn.

mixed.label: Mixed (40% cache)
mixed.description: Interactive coding with some context reuse. Typical default.

warm.label: Warm (70% cache)
warm.description: Sustained agentic session. AA's industry-standard 7:2:1 blend assumes this rate. danielvaughan.com's Codex CLI model uses 70%.

hot.label: Hot (90% cache)
hot.description: Long warmer-pattern sessions with stable system prompts and reused context. vsits.co documented 99% achievable on Claude Code Max subscription with optimization.

quota_burn_matrix.cache_methodology: Cache discount = (cache_hit_price / input_price). Lower = better discount. Effective burn = (1 - cache_hit_rate) × raw_burn + cache_hit_rate × raw_burn × cache_discount. Per-provider cache discounts: Anthropic 10% (90% off), OpenAI 25% on flagship/10% on 5.4 family, DeepSeek V4-Pro 0.83% (99.2% off — most aggressive in industry), DeepSeek V4-Flash 2%, MiniMax 52%, Mistral & Grok no published cache pricing (modeled as 100% = no discount), Google ~25% plus per-hour storage fee (not modeled). IMPORTANT CAVEAT on Anthropic subscriptions: docs say cache reads count at 10% rate, but GitHub issue anthropics/claude-code#24147 reports cache reads burning quota at full rate on Max subscriptions. Treat the displayed cache benefit as accurate for API usage; subscription quota behavior is contested. Cache writes (1.25× / 2× of input rate on Anthropic) are not modeled — amortize across many reads in steady-state.

sources.title: SWE-bench (Verified)
sources.note: SWE-bench Verified is widely considered contaminated; treat with skepticism.

sources.title: AIME (math competition benchmark)
sources.note: Used to anchor Reasoning axis ratings.

sources.title: MMLU / MMLU-Pro (knowledge benchmark)
sources.note: Used to anchor Knowledge axis ratings.

sources.title: tau-bench (tool-use + agentic behaviour)
sources.note: Used to anchor Agentic axis ratings.

sources.title: Contabo GPU Cloud configurations and pricing

capabilities.methodology: 1-5 levels per axis; Coding rated against SWE-Pro / SWE-Verified evidence; Reasoning against AIME / LiveCodeBench / public hard-reasoning evals; Multimodal against published modality support (image/audio/video in/out); Agentic rated holding harness constant at Claude-Code-class baseline (plan coherence, tool-call quality, self-correction, calibrated stopping, refusal hygiene); Knowledge from model-card claims + community-validated state-of-art awareness; Comms from prose-evaluation rounds + structural quality. Ratings carry citation; see src/dashboard-context.md for the full rubric.
capabilities.effort_formula: effective(axis, effort) = max(0, ceiling[axis] × (1 − effort_sensitivity[axis] × (1 − radar_effort_factor[effort]))). radar_effort_factor[medium] = 1.00 → primary polygon equals reference polygon shape at medium effort for the same model. Lower efforts shrink the polygon by sensitivity-weighted amounts; higher efforts grow it and may push vertices OUTSIDE the outer level-50 ring — the ring is a rubric marker, not a hard cap. Vertices that exceed 50 are drawn in accent-hot to signal 'boosted above medium-effort baseline'.

scale.interpretation: capability_levels[axis] = the model's effective capability at MEDIUM effort. Briefings rate from 1 to 50. The radar's visual scale extends to 60 to accommodate the 'effort-boost band' (51–60) — values that fall there are formula-derived from extra reasoning effort, never directly rated. The outer rubric ring at 50 is visually emphasized as 'current frontier'.
